questions.q: Your friend is having eye sight problem. She is not able to see clearly a distant uniform window mesh and it appears to her as nonuniform and distorted. The doctor diagnosed the problem as :
questions.solution: If distant objects are blurry then the problem is Myopia. If objects are distorted then the problem is Astigmatism

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For a concave lens of focal length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math>, the relation between object and image distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mo>,</mo></math> respectively, from its pole can best be represented by (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>v</mi></math> is the reference line):
questions.solution:
From the lens formula, 1v-1u=1f

⇒v=f-f2u+f

 now, if we plot a graph between u and v 

When u→∞; v=f-f2∞=f

and for u→0; v=0

Given that u=v is a reference line, therefore, dvdu=0+f2u+f2

dvduu=0=0+f20+f2=1

The equation of tangent of the curve at the origin, v-0=1u-0⇒v=u

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The angle of deviation through a prism is minimum when 

(A) Incident ray and emergent ray are symmetric to the prism

(B) The refracted ray inside the prism becomes parallel to its base

(C) Angle of incidence is equal to that of the angle of emergence

(D) When angle of emergence is double the angle of incidence

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
As per the figure given in question, we are considering it an Isosceles Prism.

Deviation is minimum in a prism when :

i=e,r1=r2 and ray 2 is parallel to base of prism.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Time taken by light to travel in two different materials <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> of refractive indices <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi>A</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi>B</mi></msub></math> of same thickness is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> respectively. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> and the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi>A</mi></msub></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi>B</mi></msub></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn></math>. Then the thickness of material, in meter is: (Given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mi>A</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mi>B</mi></msub></math> are velocities of light in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> materials respectively).
questions.solution:
The speed of light in a medium is inversely proportional to the refractive index of the material. Therefore,

μAμB=vBvA=12

Let the thickness is d

⇒t2-t1=dvB-dvA=5×10-10 s

As vA=2vB⇒d=5×10-10×2vB

⇒d=5×10-10×vA m.

questions.q: Two plane mirrors M1 and M2 are at right angle to each other shown. A point source P is placed at a and 2a meter away from M1 and M2 respectively. The shortest distance between the images thus formed is : (Take 5=2.3)
questions.solution: We know that the formula of the distance between two points is, d=x2-x12+y2-y12, now put the values from the above the diagram in the above formula, then the distance between I1 and I2, d=(4a)2+(2a)2≈4.6a

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A convex lens has power P. It is cut into two halves along its principal axis. Further one piece (out of the two halves) is cut into two halves perpendicular to the principal axis (as shown in figures). Choose the incorrect option for the reported pieces.
questions.solution:
Using Lens maker's formula, power is given by

P=1f=μ-11R1-1R2

 

When convex lens is cut into two parts, P1=1f=μ-12R=P

 

When half convex lens is cut into two parts,P2=1f=μ-11R=P2

Power of lens,  P3=1f=μ-11R=P2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> meter long scale with least count of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> is used to measure the locations of objects on an optical bench. While measuring the focal length of a convex lens, the object pin and the convex lens are placed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> mark and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> mark, respectively. The image of the object pin on the other side of lens coincides with image pin that is kept at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> mark. The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>%</mo></math> error in the estimation of focal length is:
questions.solution:
The formula for a lens is given by 1f=1v-1u   ...(i)

u=100±0.2 cm-80±0.2 cm=20±0.4 cm

v=180±0.2 cm-100±0.2 cm=80±0.4 cm

Substituting the values in the equation of focal length,

1f=1v-1u=180--120=580

⇒f=16 cm

Differentiating the equation (i)

dff2=dvv2+duu2(Only positive sign is considered for error calculation)

=0.46400+0.4400=0.4×176400

⇒df=0.4×176400×162=0.272

The percentage error is 

dff×100=0.27216×100=1.70%

If the wavelength of light is changed to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math>, the number of fringes observed in the same segment of the screen would be :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An ice cube has a bubble inside. When viewed from one side the apparent distance of the bubble is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. When viewed from the opposite side, the apparent distance of the bubble is observed as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. If the side of the ice cube is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>, the refractive index of the ice cube is
questions.solution:
Let's assume that the original position of the bubble is x from one side of the ice cube of side a.

When the bubble is seen from one side, it can be written that

xμ=12   ...1

For the view from the other side, it can be written that

a-xμ=4   ...2

From equations (1) and (2), it is given that

24-12μ=4μ⇒μ=2416=1.5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A beam of plane polarized light of large cross-sectional area and uniform intensity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.3</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> falls normally on a polarizer (cross-sectional area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>), which rotates about its axis with an angular speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>31</mn><mo>.4</mo><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The energy of light passing through the polarizer per revolution, is close to:
questions.solution:
Given,

Intensity of plane polarized light of large cross-sectional area is I0=3.3 W m-2

Area of cross-section of polarizer is A=3×10-4 m2

Angular speed of rotation is ω=31.4 rad s-1

The intensity of light is the given by the definition,

 I=EAT  ...(1)

Intensity of light passing through the polarizer as polarizer rotates is given by

Iavg=I01T∫0Tcos2ωtdt

⇒Iavg=I01T∫0T1+cos2ωt2dt

⇒Iavg=I012T∫0Tdt+∫0Tcos2ωtdt

⇒Iavg=I012TT+0

⇒Iavg=I02  ...(2)

Using equation (1) and (2) we get energy per revolution is given by

E=I0A2T=I0A22πω

E=3.3×3×10−423.1431.4

E≃1×10-4 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two coherent sources of light interfere. The intensity ratio of two sources is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>4</mn></math>. For this interference pattern if the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>I</mi><mi>max</mi></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>I</mi><mi>min</mi></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>I</mi><mi>max</mi></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>I</mi><mi>min</mi></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></mfrac></math> will be
questions.solution:
Given here, I1I2=14 or I2=4I1

Maximum intensity is Imax=I1+I22=I1+4I1+2I14I1=9I1

Minimum intensity is Imin=I1-I22=I1+4I1-2I14I1=I1

The ratio of Imax+IminImax-Imin is 

 9I1+I19I1-I1=108=54=2α+1β+3

α=2  β=1

∴αβ=21=2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math>’ polarizing sheets are arranged such that each makes an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the proceeding sheet. An unpolarized light of intensity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> is incident into this arrangement. The output intensity is found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>I</mi><mn>64</mn></mfrac></math> . The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> will be:
questions.solution:
When an unpolarized light passes through a polariser, the intensity of the emergent light from the polariser depends on the square of the cosine of the angle between the plane of the polariser and the direction of the incident light.

The formula to calculate the intensity I1 of the emergent light from a polariser which makes an angle θ with the direction of the incident light is given by

I1= Icos2θ..................(1)

where, I is the intensity of the incident light.

Substitute 45° for θ into equation (1) to obtain the intensity of the first emergent light.

I1= Icos245°= I2

In a similar manner, the intensity I2 of the second emergent light can be written as

I2= I1cos245°= I12= I4=I22

Thus, for n number of such polarisers, the net intensity In of the emergent light can be written as

In= I2n......................(1)

Comparing equation (1) with the given expression for the intensity, it can be concluded that

n= 6
